question: Under the pinned production-night model (budget B=12 units, size leg B=6 reporting-only; new title costs 1, each extra version costs c in {0.25, 0.5, 0.75}; policy K in {1,2,3,4,6} versions per title, T_eff = B/(1+c*(K-1)) fractional by linearity of expectation; title appeal theta ~ N(0,1), version quality theta+eps with eps ~ N(0, sigma_v^2), sigma_v in {0.2, 0.5, 1.0}; revenue exp(q+L) in relative units with lottery L ~ N(-sigma_m^2/2, sigma_m^2) so E[exp(L)]=1, sigma_m in {0.5, 1.5, 2.5}; Mode P pick-best publishes 1 of K with true-best picked at fidelity f in {0.2, 0.6, 1.0} else uniform; Mode A publish-all lists all K with R = (1-s)*max_i r_i + s*sum_i r_i at audience separation s in {0, 0.5, 1}), what is K* = argmax_K E[revenue per unit budget] and dR = R(K*)/R(1) - 1 per cell (Mode P 81 cells c x sigma_v x sigma_m x f, Mode A 81 cells c x sigma_v x sigma_m x s) - in BOTH Arm A (analytic, seedless: the Mode P f=1 slice via Simpson quadrature of integral exp(sigma_v*x)*K*phi(x)*Phi(x)^(K-1) dx over [-10,10] step 0.001, stdlib math.erf) and Arm S (seeded MC: M=20,000 titles per (mode, cell, K); random.Random(20260716) Mode P / random.Random(20260717) Mode A in a pinned loop order; decision-stability leg M=2,000 seed 20260718 must reproduce the same ruling; Arm S must agree with Arm A within 1.5% on every f=1 cell or the run is invalid; hit-probability legs at integer T=floor(T_eff), B in {6,12}, hit := within-cell p90 of the K=1 revenue distribution, reporting-only) - and does the result land APPROVE, REJECT, or NULL per the pre-registered rule?

source.proposal_pin: idea-engine main d0dca70 (landed via idea-engine PR #283)
source.grounding: venture-lab control/inbox.md ORDER 008 @ 81c47ec (the owner's BOOKS night-run directive, verbatim in the idea file)
source.hermetic: fully hermetic (the PROPOSAL 017 precedent) - every fixture below is a pinned constant copied verbatim from the idea file; zero repo/network reads in the verdict session

quadrature.rule: composite Simpson, stdlib math.erf for Phi

bands.APPROVE: in BOTH modes: (K* >= 2 in >= 80% of cells) AND (median dR >= +0.10)
bands.REJECT: in BOTH modes: K* = 1 in >= 80% of cells
bands.NULL: anything else (modes disagree, or the K* mass straddles) - a legitimate reportable outcome: the verdict names the flip axis via per-value K*>=2 cell-shares (largest spread = the named boundary; expected candidates: audience separation s and selection fidelity f)
bands.median_definition: sorted ascending, element at 0-based index 40 of the 81 per-cell dR values (81 odd - exact)

pinned_loop_order.cells: lexicographic over (c, sigma_v, sigma_m, f) for Mode P and (c, sigma_v, sigma_m, s) for Mode A, each axis ascending in its grid order above; K ascending within a cell; titles sequential within a (cell, K) leg
pinned_loop_order.mode_P_per_title_draws: theta = rng.normalvariate(0,1); eps_1..eps_K = rng.normalvariate(0, sigma_v) each; u_pick = rng.random(); j_uniform = rng.randrange(K) (always drawn, consumed only when u_pick >= f); L = rng.normalvariate(-sigma_m^2/2, sigma_m)
pinned_loop_order.mode_A_per_title_draws: theta = rng.normalvariate(0,1); eps_1..eps_K = rng.normalvariate(0, sigma_v) each; L_1..L_K = rng.normalvariate(-sigma_m^2/2, sigma_m) each
pinned_loop_order.stability_leg: ONE random.Random(20260718) consumed sequentially: full Mode P grid first, then full Mode A grid, same per-title draw order; decision-level comparison only
pinned_loop_order.pick_rule: picked = argmax_i eps_i (first index on exact float ties) if u_pick < f else j_uniform
pinned_loop_order.kstar_tie_rule: argmax over K_grid ascending; strictly-greater wins, so the smallest K wins exact ties

estimators_disclosed.note: the pre-registered spec pins the model, grids, seeds, M, and per-title draw order; the mean ESTIMATOR is sim-internal. Both modes use unbiased conditional-expectation (Rao-Blackwell) / control-variate estimators over exactly the pinned draws - required to make the pre-registered 1.5% Arm A/S gate satisfiable at M=20,000 (the raw exp(q+L) sample mean has relative SE ~26% at sigma_m=2.5 and can never pass it). The raw exp(q+L) revenue draws are still made in the pinned order and are used as-is for the distributional hit-probability legs.
estimators_disclosed.mode_P_value_estimator: Xhat = exp(1/2) * [ f * A(eps) + (1-f) * exp(sigma_v^2/2) ], with A(eps) = (1/2) * [ (K*exp(sigma_v^2/2) - (sum_i exp(eps_i) - max_i exp(eps_i))) + (K*exp(sigma_v^2/2) - (sum_i exp(-eps_i) - max_i exp(-eps_i))) ]  (theta, L, the pick draw, and the uniform-pick branch integrated out analytically - E[exp(theta)]=exp(1/2), E[exp(L)]=1, E[exp(eps_uniform)]=exp(sigma_v^2/2); A is the sign-flip-antithetic beta=1 control variate for E[exp(max eps)] on the exact identity E[sum_i exp(eps_i)] = K*exp(sigma_v^2/2), valid by N(0,sigma_v) symmetry; zero-variance at K=1)
estimators_disclosed.mode_A_value_estimator: Xhat = exp(1/2) * [ K*exp(sigma_v^2/2) - (1-s) * sum_{i != argmax g} g_i ]  with g_i = exp(eps_i + L_i)  (theta integrated out analytically; beta=1 control variate on the exact identity E[sum_i g_i] = K*exp(sigma_v^2/2), so R = (1-s)*max g + s*sum g is estimated with only the loser-sum as residual noise; zero-variance at K=1)
estimators_disclosed.unbiasedness: both are exact conditional expectations / linear control variates of the pinned model's revenue - E[Xhat] = E[R_title] cell-for-cell; self-checked against the analytic diagnostic layer below
estimators_disclosed.analytic_diagnostic_layer: beyond the pre-registered Arm A slice, the pinned model's full mean structure is closed-form via the same quadrature: Mode P E[R_title] = exp(1/2)*(f*I(K,sigma_v) + (1-f)*exp(sigma_v^2/2)); Mode A E[R_title] = exp(1/2)*((1-s)*exp(-sigma_m^2/2)*I(K,sqrt(sigma_v^2+sigma_m^2)) + s*K*exp(sigma_v^2/2)), with I(K,sigma) = integral exp(sigma*x)*K*phi(x)*Phi(x)^(K-1) dx. Used as SELF-CHECKS and reported; the pre-registered arms and ruling are unchanged (ruling from the Arm S grids, Arm A validating the f=1 slice).

hit_probability_legs.definition: reporting-only, cannot flip the decision: per (mode, cell, K, B) with B in {6,12}, T = floor(B/(1+c*(K-1))), p_single = fraction of the leg's M raw title revenues strictly greater than the within-cell p90 of the K=1 raw revenue sample, P_hit = 1 - (1-p_single)^T
hit_probability_legs.p90_definition: nearest-rank: sorted ascending, element at index ceil(0.9*M)-1
hit_probability_legs.raw_revenue: Mode P: exp(theta + eps_picked + L); Mode A: exp(theta) * ((1-s)*max_i g_i + s*sum_i g_i)

caveats_pinned.elasticity: the exp link fixes quality elasticity at 1, bracketed by the sigma_m sweep
caveats_pinned.relative_units: this sim allocates effort, it never forecasts earnings - Q-0259 r.4 forecast discipline untouched
caveats_pinned.sigma_m_mode_P: sigma_m is provably inert in Mode P MEANS (E[exp(L)]=1 by construction, L independent of the pick) - it enters Mode P only through the distributional hit legs; stated up front, verified by self-check

cpython_pin: CPython 3.11 (results table pinned to 3.11.x; random.Random / normalvariate stream semantics)
